title: Regeneration Hash Manifest
description: Per-section content-hash record for a regenerable output artifact. Implements Pattern XII of the Integral Knowledge Architecture methodology — hash-manifest incremental regeneration.

artifact_format.description: Output format. The regeneration pipeline must support splicing at section boundaries for the chosen format.

source_id.description: Identifier for the source content this artifact regenerates from (e.g., source article path, source bundle ID).

source_language.description: BCP 47 language code for the source content.

generator.description: What produced this artifact. Provider identification supports per-provider cost auditing and quality tracking.

provider.description: External provider used by the generator (e.g., 'xai-grok-tts', 'system').

sections.description: Per-section hash records. Order MUST match the artifact's section order.

last_full_regeneration.description: Timestamp of the most recent full regeneration (e.g., for force-rebuilds, voice changes).

section_id.description: Stable identifier for this section (typically a slug or numeric index). MUST remain stable across regenerations to enable splicing.

source_hash.description: SHA-256 (or truncated SHA-256) of this section's source content at the time of last generation. Comparison against current source hash determines whether regeneration is needed.

output_offset.description: Byte offset (for audio/video) or character offset (for text) of this section's start in the output artifact. Enables splicing without re-reading the entire artifact.

output_duration_ms.description: For audio/video: duration of this section in milliseconds.

generated_at.description: When this specific section was last generated.

skipped_reason.description: Optional: when set, indicates this section was deliberately skipped on last regeneration (e.g., 'hash unchanged'). Audit trail for the regeneration discipline.
